title: Competitor feature/price scan
acceptance_criteria: Covers at minimum these 6 competitors: CoinSignals (or equivalent retail crypto signal service), TradingView alerts + paid indicators, Arkham Intelligence, Nansen, Dune Analytics (query layer), whalemap.io

For each competitor, a row with columns: name, URL, primary product, pricing (Free / Starter / Pro / Enterprise tiers with $/mo), signals per day or API calls, latency (real-time vs delayed), symbol coverage, API/webhook availability, key differentiator, our differentiator vs them
Pricing pulled from public pricing pages as of the doc date; note 'checked YYYY-MM-DD' at top
Our differentiator column specifically: what do we have that they do not. Bittensor-consensus signals is the honest answer for most; multi-strategy 13-agent ensemble for others

Bottom-of-doc section: Positioning Recommendations — 3-5 bullets on how our pricing and feature mix should respond to the competitive set (e.g., 'Trader $49 undercuts TradingView Premium at $60', 'Enterprise $199 is above retail signal services but well below Nansen at $150-$1800')
No fabricated data; if a competitor's pricing is not publicly listed, write 'Private / contact sales' rather than guessing
verification_note: Opencode drafts from public pricing pages via WebFetch. User spot-checks 2 competitors before accepting the PR to catch hallucinations.
